name: metaphor-connectors

metadata.name: metaphor-connectors
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A collection of Python-based 'connectors' that extract metadata from various sources to ingest into the Metaphor app.

documentation: # Metaphor Connectors [![Codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/MetaphorData/connectors)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/MetaphorData/connectors/tree/main) [![CodeQL](https://github.com/MetaphorData/connectors/workflows/CodeQL/badge.svg)](https://github.com/MetaphorData/connectors/actions/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml) [![PyPI Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/metaphor-connectors)](https://pypi.org/project/metaphor-connectors/) ![Python version 3.9+](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.9%2B-blue) ![PyPI Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/metaphor-connectors) [![Docker Pulls](https://img.shields.io/docker/pulls/metaphordata/connectors)](https://hub.docker.com/r/metaphordata/connectors) [![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/MetaphorData/connectors)](https://github.com/MetaphorData/connectors/blob/master/LICENSE) This repository contains a collection of Python-based "connectors" that extract metadata from various sources to ingest into the [Metaphor](https://metaphor.io) platform. ## Installation This package requires Python 3.9+ installed. You can verify the version on your system by running the following command, ```shell python -V # or python3 on some systems ``` Once verified, you can install the package using [pip](https://docs.python.org/3/installing/index.html), ```shell pip install "metaphor-connectors[all]" # or pip3 on some systems ``` This will install all the connectors and required dependencies. You can also choose to install only a subset of the dependencies by installing the specific [extra](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/installing-packages/#installing-setuptools-extras), e.g. ```shell pip install "metaphor-connectors[snowflake]" ``` Similarly, you can also install the package using `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`. ## Docker We automatically push a [docker image](https://hub.docker.com/r/metaphordata/connectors) to Docker Hub as part of the CI/CD. See [this page](./docs/docker.md) for more details. ## GitHub Action You can also run the connectors in your CI/CD pipeline using the [Metaphor Connectors](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/metaphor-connectors-github-action) GitHub Action. ## Connectors Each connector is placed under its own directory under [metaphor](./metaphor) and extends the `metaphor.common.BaseExtractor` class. | Connector Name | Metadata | |-------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------| | [athena](metaphor/athena/) | Schema, description, queries | | [azure_data_factory](metaphor/azure_data_factory/) | Lineage, Pipeline | | [bigquery](metaphor/bigquery/) | Schema, description, statistics, queries | | [bigquery.lineage](metaphor/bigquery/lineage/) | Lineage | | [bigquery.profile](metaphor/bigquery/profile/) | Data profile | | [confluence](metaphor/confluence/) | Document embeddings | | [custom.data_quality](metaphor/custom/data_quality/) | Data quality | | [custom.governance](metaphor/custom/governance/) | Ownership, tags, description | | [custom.lineage](metaphor/custom/lineage/) | Lineage | | [custom.metadata](metaphor/custom/metadata/) | Custom metadata | | [custom.query_attributions](metaphor/custom/query_attributions/) | Query attritutions | | [datahub](metaphor/datahub/) | Description, tag, ownership | | [dbt](metaphor/dbt/) | dbt model, test, lineage | | [dbt.cloud](metaphor/dbt/cloud/) | dbt model, test, lineage | | [fivetran](metaphor/fivetran/) | Lineage, Pipeline | | [glue](metaphor/glue/) | Schema, description | | [great_expectations](metaphor/great_expectations/) | Data monitor | | [informatica](metaphor/informatica/) | Lineage, Pipeline | | [looker](metaphor/looker/) | Looker view, explore, dashboard, lineage | | [kafka](metaphor/kafka/) | Schema, description | | [metabase](metaphor/metabase/) | Dashboard, lineage | | [mongodb](metaphor/mongodb/) | Schema, statistics | | [monte_carlo](metaphor/monte_carlo/) | Data monitor | | [mssql](metaphor/mssql/) | Schema | | [mysql](metaphor/mysql/) | Schema, description | | [openapi](metaphor/openapi/) | API, description | | [oracle](metaphor/oracle/) | Schema, description, queries | | [notion](metaphor/notion/) | Document embeddings | | [postgresql](metaphor/postgresql/) | Schema, description, statistics | | [postgresql.profile](metaphor/postgresql/profile/) | Data profile | | [power_bi](metaphor/power_bi/) | Dashboard, lineage | | [quick_sight](metaphor/quick_sight/) | Dashboard, lineage | | [redshift](metaphor/redshift/) | Schema, description, statistics, queries | | [redshift.profile](metaphor/redshift/profile/) | Data profile | | [s3](metaphor/s3/) | Schema, description | | [sharepoint](metaphor/sharepoint/) | Document embeddings | | [snowflake](metaphor/snowflake/) | Schema, description, statistics, queries | | [snowflake.profile](metaphor/snowflake/profile/) | Data profile | | [static_web](metaphor/static_web/) | Document embeddings | | [synapse](metaphor/synapse/) | Schema, queries | | [tableau](metaphor/tableau/) | Dashboard, lineage | | [thought_spot](metaphor/thought_spot/) | Dashboard, lineage | | [trino](metaphor/trino/) | Schema, description, queries | | [unity_catalog](metaphor/unity_catalog/) | Schema, description | | [unity_catalog.profile](metaphor/unity_catalog/profile/) | Data profile, statistics | ## Development See [Development Environment](docs/develop.md) for more instructions on how to set up your local development environment. ## Custom Connectors See [Adding a Custom Connector](docs/custom.md) for instructions and a full example of creating your custom connectors.

classes.name: metaphor.kafka.extractor.KafkaExtractor
documentation: Kafka metadata extractor

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_resolver.SchemaResolver._resolve_topic_to_subjects
documentation: Returns the list of subjects that relates to the topic.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_resolver.SchemaResolver._resolve_topic_naming_strategy
documentation:
Resolves the topic to a tuple of records and SubjectNameStrategy.
If the topic isn't in `self._topic_naming_strategies`, returns an empty list and the default SubjectNameStrategy.

classes.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.protobuf_parser.ProtobufDataTypes
documentation:
Enum for Protobuf Datatypes.

Reference:
https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/blob/main/python/google/protobuf/descriptor.py#L485

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.protobuf_parser.ProtobufParser._get_protobuf_python_object
documentation:
Compiles the protobuf schema to a Python file, then import the Python
file dynamically to determine the top level message object.

The top level message name is parsed internally. If there are more than
one possible top level message, we use the first message type that isn't
referenced by any other ones as our top level message.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.protobuf_parser.ProtobufParser._find_top_level_message
documentation:
Go through the entire generated module, and see if we can find a message
type that isn't referenced by any other message type. If there is such a
message, we use it as our top level message type.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser._parse_array_child
documentation:
Returns the type in the array along with the list of schema fields for the underlying
complex types.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser._parse_record_child
documentation: Parses the record schema into a schema field.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser._parse_union_children
documentation:
Returns the union types along with the list of schema fields for the underlying
complex types.

Note that avro specification for union schemas is that an union cannot immediately
contain another union, so here we do not handle that.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser.parse_array_fields
metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser.parse_record_fields
documentation: Parse the nested record fields for avro

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser.parse_union_fields
documentation:
Parse union field for avro schema.

Here we populate the schema field for the union schema, and we'll parse
the underlying types (arrays or records) in `_parse_union_children`.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser.parse_single_field
documentation: Parses primitive fields.

methods.name: metaphor.kafka.schema_parsers.avro_parser.AvroParser.get_avro_fields
documentation: The main recursion. Recursively convert parsed schemas into schema field models.

classes.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSubjectNameStrategy

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSubjectNameStrategy.TOPIC_NAME_STRATEGY
documentation:
The default strategy. Maps a topic to a single subject for its key and a single subject for its value.

Resulting subject:
`<TOPIC>-<key|value>`

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSubjectNameStrategy.RECORD_NAME_STRATEGY
documentation:
Maps a topic to multiple fully qualified records, thus allowing multiple schemas for a single topic.

To use this strategy, user will need to specify the records related to the topic.

Resulting subject:
`<RECORD>-<key|value>`

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSubjectNameStrategy.TOPIC_RECORD_NAME_STRATEGY
documentation:
Maps a topic to multiple fully qualified records and prefixes the record with the topic name.

If user does not specify the records related to the topic, we will use whatever subjects we find
that starts with `topic` and ends with `key|value` as the related subjects.

Resulting subject:
`<TOPIC>-<RECORD>-<key|value>`
The naming strategy from topic to subject.

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaTopicNamingStrategy.override_subject_name_strategy
documentation:
This will be resolved in `SchemaResolver` so that we always know what strategy we're
using for this topic.

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaTopicNamingStrategy.records
documentation:
If the subject name strategy is `TopicNameStrategy`, this will be ignored.

If the subject name strategy is `RecordNameStrategy`, this is mandatory for the topic to be
correctly ingested.

If the subject name strategy is `TopicRecordNameStrategy` and `records` is empty / not defined,
all subjects that starts with topic and ends with `key|value` will be considered as a subject for
this topic.

classes.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSASLConfig

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSASLConfig.username
documentation:
SASL username for use with the `PLAIN` and `SASL-SCRAM-..` mechanisms.

Config key: "sasl.username".

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSASLConfig.password
documentation:
SASL password for use with the `PLAIN` and `SASL-SCRAM-..` mechanisms.

Config key: "sasl.password".

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaSASLConfig.mechanism
documentation:
SASL mechanism to use for authentication. Supported: `GSSAPI`, `PLAIN`, `SCRAM-SHA-256`, `SCRAM-SHA-512`, `OAUTHBEARER`.

Config key: "sasl.mechanism".
The most commonly used SASL configuration values. For other configurations, please use `extra_admin_client_config` field.

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaConfig.schema_registry_url
documentation:
Schema registry URL.

Can contain basic HTTP auth username and password, e.g.
```
http://username:password@host:port
```

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaConfig.extra_admin_client_conf
documentation:
Extra configuration values for the Kafka admin client. See https://github.com/confluentinc/librdkafka/blob/master/CONFIGURATION.md
for all available configurations.

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaConfig.bootstrap_servers
documentation: The Kafka bootstrap servers / brokers. Cannot be empty.

class_variables.name: metaphor.kafka.config.KafkaConfig.topic_naming_strategies
documentation: Mapping from topic name to corresponding naming strategy.

class_variables.name: metaphor.database.config.GenericDatabaseRunConfig.host
documentation: host of the database the crawler will connect to

class_variables.name: metaphor.database.config.GenericDatabaseRunConfig.port
documentation: Use MySQL port here, subclass should overwrite this

documentation: Extracts and returns visible text from given HTML content as a single string.

classes.name: metaphor.static_web.extractor.StaticWebExtractor

methods.name: metaphor.static_web.extractor.StaticWebExtractor._check_page_make_document
documentation:
Gets a page's HTML and adds to the visited pages set.
If page has valid content, extracts the text and title and generates
a Document object for the page.

Returns a bool and the page_content:
    out[0]: False if the page content is invalid, True otherwise.
    out[1]: "" if the page content is invalid, page_content otherwise

methods.name: metaphor.static_web.extractor.StaticWebExtractor._get_page_HTML
documentation: Fetches a webpage's content, returning an error message on failure.

methods.name: metaphor.static_web.extractor.StaticWebExtractor._get_subpages_from_HTML
documentation:
Extracts and returns a list of subpage URLs from a given page's HTML and URL.
Subpage URLs are reconstructed to be absolute URLs and anchor links are trimmed.

methods.name: metaphor.static_web.extractor.StaticWebExtractor._make_document
documentation:
Constructs Document objects from webpage URLs
and their content, including extra metadata.

Cleans text content and includes data like page title,
platform URL, page link, refresh timestamp, and page ID.
Static webpage extractor.

methods.name: metaphor.tableau.extractor.TableauExtractor._build_asset_full_name_and_structure

- openapi

- Great Expectations

- nifi

classes.name: metaphor.great_expectations.extractor.GreatExpectationsExtractor

methods.name: metaphor.great_expectations.extractor.GreatExpectationsExtractor._extract_database_from_sqlalchemy_url
documentation:
Reference:
https://docs.greatexpectations.io/docs/core/connect_to_data/sql_data/#procedure

Great Expectations metadata extractor. The extractor runs by
parsing existing Great Expectations data context, so make sure the
execution context has been persisted. In other words, it will not
work if you get Great Expectations context like this in your script:
```python
ctx = gx.get_context() # This creates a context in memory, and nothing is persisted
```

methods.name: metaphor.monday.extractor.MondayExtractor._get_board_items

Retrieves max_items items from specified board.
If consume == True and a cursor is present,

methods.name: metaphor.monday.extractor.MondayExtractor._construct_items_documents
documentation:
Constructs Document objects from a list of items and column metadata.
If a column contains a Monday document, calls get_monday_doc() to

Monday.com Items extractor.

classes.name: metaphor.common.base_config.BaseConfig

class_variables.name: metaphor.common.base_config.BaseConfig.output
documentation:
No default value here, otherwise dataclass inheritance would not work.

If this field is `None`, the connector will store the extracted data to
`PWD`/`timestamp`.

To disable storing data altogether, set this field to `OutputConfig()`.

Base class for runtime parameters

All subclasses should add the @dataclass decorators

functions.name: metaphor.common.utils.chunk_by_size
documentation:
Split a list into chunks based on specified limits

Normally each chunk is packed with as many successive items as possible
without exceeding items_per_chunk & chunk_size. However, if a single item
is larger than chunk_size, it'll be put into its own chunk.

Parameters
----------
items_per_chunk:
    Maximum number of items in a chunk
list_to_chunk : list
    The list to be chunked
chunk_size : int
    The ideal size of a chunk
size_func : Callabale
    A function that computes the size of each item

Returns
-------
list
    a list of slices, each represent a chunk

functions.name: metaphor.common.utils.filter_none
documentation: Filter out all None values from the list

functions.name: metaphor.common.utils.to_utc_datetime_from_timestamp
documentation: Return a datetime from timestamp, None if timestamp are None

variables.name: metaphor.common.sql.table_level_lineage.helpers.expression_handlers.expression_handlers
documentation:
Some expressions don't have SELECT in them but they still have
lineage that we want to know. We parse these expressions separately.

variables.name: metaphor.common.sql.dialect.PLATFORM_TO_DIALECT
documentation: Mapping from data platforms supported by Metaphor to dialects recognized by SQLGlot.

functions.name: metaphor.common.sql.process_query.bad_queries.is_bad_query_pattern
documentation:
There are some sql expressions that SQLGlot cannot parse, but our data processing
engine emits them. When we encounter these kinds of expressions, just ignore them.

variables.name: metaphor.common.sql.process_query.bad_queries._DESCRIBE_EXTENDED_COLUMN_STATS
documentation: This is for `DESCRIBE EXTENDED table_name column_name`.

functions.name: metaphor.common.sql.process_query.process_query.process_query
documentation:
Processes a crawled SQL query.

Parameters
----------
sql : str
    The SQL query to process.

data_platform : DataPlatform
    The data platform. If not supported, the query will not be processed.

config : ProcessQueryConfig
    Config for controlling what to do.

query_id : str
    The ID of the SQL query.

Returns
-------
The processed SQL query as a `str`, or `None` if the SQL query does not
include any lineage.

functions.name: metaphor.common.variable.variable_substitution
documentation:
Recursively substitute strings with variable expression in the dictionary

A variable expression is in the form of ${VAR_NAME}. Its value will
be substitute with the value of a matching environment variable name.
If no matching environment variable is found, the expression will be
left as-is.

File sink functions

functions.name: metaphor.common.runner.run_connector
documentation:
Run a connector and write the resulting events to files and/or API.

Parameters
----------
make_connector : Callable[[], BaseExtractor]
    The function to create and return an instance of connector
name : str
    Name of the connector
description : str
    Textual description of the connector
platform : Optional[Platform]
    Platform of the connector
file_sink_config : Optional[FileSinkConfig]
    Optional configuration for outputting events to files or cloud storage

Returns
-------
list
    a list of MetadataChangeEvent written to the file sink

functions.name: metaphor.common.runner.metaphor_file_sink_config
documentation:
Create a FileSinkConfig for outputting events to a Metaphor tenant's cloud storage

Parameters
----------
tenant : str
    Name of the tenant
connector_name : str
    Name of the connector
is_metaphor_cloud : bool
    Whether this is a Metaphor cloud tenant (default is False)

Returns
-------
FileSinkConfig
    the config created

functions.name: metaphor.common.runner.local_file_sink_config
documentation:
Create a FileSinkConfig for outputting events to a local directory

Parameters
----------
directory : str
    Path to a local directory

Returns
-------
FileSinkConfig
    the config created

functions.name: metaphor.common.embeddings.sanitize_text
documentation:
Takes an input string and applies the following manipulations:
* newline characters replaced with a single space
* tab characters replaced with a single space
* carriage return characters replaced with a single space
* multiple spaces replaced with a single space
* leading / trailing whitespace removed

Returns a string.

functions.name: metaphor.common.embeddings.embed_documents
documentation:
Generates embeddings for Documents and returns them as stored in a
VectorStoreIndex object. Has configurable chunk
and overlap sizes for node generation from Documents.

Returns a VectorStoreIndex (in-memory VectorStore)

functions.name: metaphor.common.embeddings.map_metadata
documentation:
Takes the embedding_dict, metadata_dict, and doc_store from
a VectorStoreIndex's to_dict() method. Outputs a list of
externalSearchDocuments that matches the document ingestion schema.

Returns a list of nodes, represented as dictionaries.

variables.name: metaphor.unity_catalog.utils.SPECIAL_CHARACTERS
documentation:
The special characters mentioned in Databricks documentation are:
- `
- `-`

The following characters are not allowed:
- `.`
- ` `
- `/`

See https://docs.databricks.com/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-names.html for reference.

`STREAM` is there because information schema doesn't know about this type.

name.

is unable to process the query if it is over 65535 characters long.

classes.name: metaphor.s3.table_data.TableData

methods.name: metaphor.s3.table_data.TableData.merge
documentation:
Merges this TableData with another one.

The merge only makes sense if the table_paths are equivalent.

If there's no partition, we use the newer of the two; otherwise we merge
the partitions, and combine `size_in_bytes` and `number_of_files` of the
two TableDatas.

class_variables.name: metaphor.s3.table_data.TableData.number_of_files
documentation: TODO: make use of this field

Represents a table in the S3 storage.

User should use `table_path` as the guid.

classes.name: metaphor.s3.extractor.S3Extractor

methods.name: metaphor.s3.extractor.S3Extractor.get_dir_to_process
documentation:
Parameters
----------
bucket_name : str
    The bucket name.
folder : str
    The folder path within the bucket. Ends with a slash.
path_spec : PathSpec
    The path specification to check the folder against.

Returns
-------
Optional[str]
    The directory to process, or None if no such directory is found.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.extractor.S3Extractor._browse_path_spec
documentation:
Browses thru all eligible file objects in path spec. Resolves the wildcard characters
and labels and returns actual file paths.
S3 metadata extractor

classes.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PartitionField

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PartitionField.inferred_type
documentation:
Infer partition column type from raw value.

There should be a more robust way to do this, but for now this will
be sufficient.

class_variables.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PartitionField.name
documentation:
Name of the partition column. For unnamed columns, this will be
`Unnamed column {index}`.
Represents a partition column.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.bucket
documentation: For uri = `s3://foo/bar/baz.csv`, this would be `foo`.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.object_path
documentation: For uri = `s3://foo/bar/baz.csv`, this would be `bar/baz.csv`.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.full_path
documentation: For uri = `s3://foo/bar/baz.csv`, this would be `/foo/bar/baz.csv`.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.path_prefix
documentation:
Returns the prefix of the path before the label or wildcard levels. E.g.
for uri = `s3://foo/bar/baz/*/*/{table}/*/*.*`, this would be
`bar/baz/`.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.labels
documentation:
The labels in the uri. Below are the rules for different labels:

- `{table}`
    - The table label. The directories at the level of this label will
      be treated as a dataset. This label can appear at most once in the
      uri. If there are any other kinds of labels, this label must exist.
- `{partition[N]}` & `{partition_key[N]}`
    - The partition labels. `{partition[N]}` are mandatory, all directories
      within that level will be treated as a partition column. If
      `{partition_key[N]}` is not provided, the partition column is considered
      an unnamed column.
- All other labels
    - Treated as part of display name, separated by `"/"`.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.partitions
documentation:
Partitions are either a single label named `{partition[N]}`, or two labels joined
by a equal sign: `{partition_key[N]}={partition[N]}`. Both formats can exist within
a single uri.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.extract_partitions
documentation: Extract the partition columns from a file uri.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec._partitions_are_valid
documentation: Make sure the partition labels are valid.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec._uri_is_valid
documentation: Make sure the scheme is `s3` and the bucket is provided.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec._labels_are_valid
documentation: Make sure the `{table}` label exists if there's any label within the uri.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.allow_key
documentation:
Check if the object key is accepted by this path spec's uri. Here we are comparing
against the object path.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.allow_path
documentation: Check if the directory path is accepted by this path spec.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec._path_is_excluded
documentation: Checks if a path is covered by the exclude rule.

methods.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec._trim_url_end
documentation:
Trims the final part of the URL to make sure it does not end with
either `/` or `/*`.

class_variables.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.uri
documentation:
The path specification. E.g.
`s3://foo/bar/*/{dept}/{table}/*/*.csv`

class_variables.name: metaphor.s3.path_spec.PathSpec.excludes
documentation: The excluded paths.

classes.name: metaphor.bigquery.job_change_event.JobChangeEvent

methods.name: metaphor.bigquery.job_change_event.JobChangeEvent._get_email
documentation:
Returns the email for this Job Change Event.

Paramaters
----------
service_account : bool
    If `service_account` is set to `True`, only returns the email if this email
    belongs to a service account, otherwise returns `None`.
    If `service_account` is set to `False`, only returns the email if this email
    does not belong to a service account.
